title: Multilingual read-alongs (sibling-project assessment)
context: The current project focuses on English-language read-alongs, but the literacy need is global and many PD/CC texts exist in non-English languages. Expanding to multilingual read-alongs overlaps with sibling projects (open-pronunciation-audio, decodable-readers, public-domain-translations). Before starting multilingual production, a structured assessment is needed to understand the scope, the aligner and reviewer requirements per language, the overlap with sibling projects, and whether this should be a separate project or an extension of read-aloud-audio.
objective: Assess the feasibility and scope of multilingual read-alongs, identify overlap with sibling Elyos projects, and recommend whether to extend read-aloud-audio or spin a sibling project.
acceptanceCriteria: Surveys candidate languages and source collections (e.g. Wikisource multilingual, OpenStax translations, CC children's books in target languages) with a prioritized short-list of 2–3 languages.
Identifies forced-aligner support and accuracy expectations for each candidate language (not all aligners support all languages equally).
Identifies reviewer-skill requirements for pronunciation QA in each candidate language.
Documents overlap with sibling projects (open-pronunciation-audio, decodable-readers, public-domain-translations) and proposes clear scope boundaries.
Recommends: extend read-aloud-audio with a language parameter, or launch a sibling project — with rationale.
resources: PLAN.md (non-goal: multilingual in default flow; open question on sibling projects)
Montreal Forced Aligner language model coverage
Elyos portfolio: open-pronunciation-audio, decodable-readers, public-domain-translations projects
output: A multilingual feasibility report (docs/multilingual-assessment.md), CC-BY-4.0, covering candidate languages, aligner support, reviewer requirements, sibling-project overlap, and a recommendation with rationale.
